codeReview.summary: The implementation covers the feature intent: project-scope runs remain isolated from HOME state, user-scope runs preserve pre-existing native HOME rulesync state via backup/restore, and deterministic cleanup coverage was added with real integration tests. I found one test-mode edge case that does not block the committed acceptance criteria.

issues.description: When `AF_SKIP_RULESYNC_EXEC=1` and `opts.global` are both set, `backupExistingRulesync(baseDir)` can move native HOME rulesync state to backup, but `if (skipExec) return;` exits before the `finally` block restores it. This can leave HOME state temporarily renamed during skip-exec runs (primarily test/debug mode).

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `2ebb3b2` for feature `rulesync-architecture-cleanup-and-home-isolation`. Scope-aware guard/backup behavior and new integration coverage satisfy expected behavior, with one non-blocking skip-exec restoration edge case noted.
